## 1. Introduction and Goals

> **Source analysis**: `HelloWorld.java` (5 lines), `README.md` (1 line ‚Äî project title only).

### 1.1 Purpose of This Document

This document describes the software architecture of the **HelloWorld** Java application
contained in the `copilot-test-ktruchcz` repository. Although the application is intentionally
minimal, this Arc42 documentation provides a complete, formally structured reference that
demonstrates best-practice architecture documentation and serves as a template baseline for
future growth of the project.

### 1.2 Business / Project Goals

| ID | Goal | Priority |
|----|------|----------|
| G-01 | Demonstrate a working Java program that produces visible output | High |
| G-02 | Provide a clean, compilable baseline for the `copilot-test-ktruchcz` repository | High |
| G-03 | Serve as a teaching/reference example for Java entry-point structure | Medium |
| G-04 | Act as a scaffold for future feature development | Low |

### 1.3 Quality Goals

The top quality attributes driving architectural decisions for this system are:

| Priority | Quality Attribute | Motivation |
|----------|-------------------|-----------|
| 1 | **Simplicity** | The application must be understandable in a single reading. Zero unnecessary complexity. |
| 2 | **Correctness** | The program must compile without errors and produce the exact expected output (`Hello World`). |
| 3 | **Portability** | Must run on any platform with a standard JRE ‚Äî no OS-specific dependencies. |
| 4 | **Maintainability** | Code structure must be easy to extend for future contributors. |
| 5 | **Learnability** | Acts as an onboarding artifact; any Java developer must understand it instantly. |

### 1.4 Stakeholders

| Role | Name / Group | Expectations |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| **Developer / Owner** | Repository owner (`ktruchcz`) | Working Java program, clean repo structure |
| **Future Contributors** | Open-source community | Clear entry point, easy to extend |
| **CI/CD System** | GitHub Actions (potential) | Compilable, testable source |
| **Architecture Reviewers** | Technical leads | Documented decisions and structure |
| **Learners / Students** | Java beginners | Understandable code and documentation |

## 2. Architecture Constraints

> **Source analysis**: `.gitignore` (ignores `*.class`), absence of build files, absence of
> external dependencies. All constraints inferred from project structure.

### 2.1 Technical Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Rationale / Source |
|----|------------|--------------------|
| TC-01 | **Language: Java (Standard Edition)** | Source file is `.java`; no Kotlin/Scala/Groovy interop required | 
| TC-02 | **No external libraries or frameworks** | No `pom.xml`, `build.gradle`, or `build.xml` present; all imports resolve to `java.lang` |
| TC-03 | **No build automation tool** | Neither Maven, Gradle, nor Ant is configured; compile and run must be done manually via `javac` / `java` |
| TC-04 | **Compiled artifacts excluded from VCS** | `.gitignore` explicitly excludes `*.class` files ‚Äî only source is versioned |
| TC-05 | **JDK required for compilation** | A Java Development Kit (any version ‚â• 8) must be present on the developer's machine |
| TC-06 | **JRE required for execution** | A Java Runtime Environment (any version ‚â• 8) must be present on the target machine |
| TC-07 | **Single compilation unit** | The entire application is one top-level public class (`HelloWorld`) in one file |
| TC-08 | **Default (unnamed) package** | `HelloWorld.java` declares no package, meaning it lives in the default Java package |

### 2.2 Organizational Constraints

| ID | Constraint | Rationale / Source |
|----|------------|--------------------|
| OC-01 | **GitHub-hosted repository** | Source code resides on GitHub under the `copilot-test-ktruchcz` account |
| OC-02 | **Agent-based documentation pipeline** | The `.github/agents/` directory contains agent definition files, indicating a GitHub Copilot agent workflow drives documentation generation |
| OC-03 | **No formal testing requirement (current)** | No test framework (JUnit, TestNG) is present; correctness verified by manual execution |
| OC-04 | **Open development model** | No access-control or branching strategy files detected; repository appears open |

### 2.3 Conventions

| ID | Convention | Details |
|----|------------|---------|
| CV-01 | **Java naming conventions** | Class name `HelloWorld` follows PascalCase; filename matches class name as required by Java |
| CV-02 | **Standard entry-point signature** | `public static void main(String[] args)` follows the canonical Java application entry-point contract |
| CV-03 | **4-space indentation** | Source code uses 4-space indentation (Java community standard) |
| CV-04 | **Arc42 documentation standard** | This document follows the Arc42 template structure |

---

## 3. System Scope and Context

> **Source analysis**: `HelloWorld.java` ‚Äî single `main()` method writes to `System.out`.
> No network, file system, database, or external service interactions detected.

### 3.1 Business Context

The HelloWorld application is a **standalone command-line program**. Its business context is
deliberately minimal: it receives no input from external systems, performs no data transformation
beyond encoding a string literal, and communicates its result exclusively through standard output.

| Actor / System | Relationship | Direction |
|----------------|-------------|-----------|
| **Developer** | Compiles and launches the application | ‚Üí HelloWorld |
| **JVM (Java Virtual Machine)** | Hosts and executes the application | ‚Üî HelloWorld |
| **Operating System Console (stdout)** | Receives and displays the output string | HelloWorld ‚Üí |
| **End User / Operator** | Reads the console output | ‚Üê Console |

### 3.2 C4 Context Diagram

```mermaid
C4Context
    title System Context ‚Äî HelloWorld Application

    Person(developer, "Developer", "Compiles and runs the application using JDK/JRE tools")
    Person(user, "End User / Operator", "Reads the printed output on the console")

    System(helloworld, "HelloWorld Application", "A minimal Java CLI program that prints 'Hello World' to standard output")

    System_Ext(jvm, "Java Virtual Machine (JRE)", "Provides the runtime environment: class loading, memory management, execution")
    System_Ext(os_console, "OS Console / stdout", "Terminal or shell that captures and displays the program's text output")

    Rel(developer, helloworld, "Compiles (javac) and executes (java)")
    Rel(helloworld, jvm, "Runs inside", "JVM bytecode execution")
    Rel(helloworld, os_console, "Writes 'Hello World'", "System.out.println()")
    Rel(user, os_console, "Reads output")

    UpdateLayoutConfig($c4ShapeInRow="3", $c4BoundaryInRow="1")
```

### 3.3 Technical Context

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph Developer_Machine["üñ•Ô∏è Developer Machine"]
        src["HelloWorld.java\n(source file)"]
        javac["javac compiler\n(JDK tool)"]
        bytecode["HelloWorld.class\n(bytecode ‚Äî git-ignored)"]
    end

    subgraph Runtime_Environment["‚öôÔ∏è Runtime Environment (any platform)"]
        jre["JRE / JVM"]
        stdout["stdout\n(System.out)"]
    end

    terminal["üñ•Ô∏è Terminal / Console\n(displays output)"]

    src -->|"javac HelloWorld.java"| javac
    javac -->|"produces"| bytecode
    bytecode -->|"java HelloWorld"| jre
    jre -->|"println()"| stdout
    stdout -->|"Hello World"| terminal

    style Developer_Machine fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#1e3a5f
    style Runtime_Environment fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#22c55e,color:#14532d
    style terminal fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#eab308,color:#713f12
```

### 3.4 System Boundaries

**Inside the system boundary** (what HelloWorld owns):
- The `HelloWorld` class and its `main` method
- The string literal `"Hello World"`
- The invocation of `System.out.println()`

**Outside the system boundary** (external dependencies):
- The JVM / JRE runtime
- The operating system's stdout stream
- The terminal/console rendering the output
- The `java.lang` standard library (implicitly imported)

---

## 4. Solution Strategy

> **Source analysis**: Technology choices inferred from `HelloWorld.java` language features,
> absence of build tooling, and single-class structure.

### 4.1 Technology Decisions

| Decision | Choice Made | Alternatives Considered | Rationale |
|----------|------------|------------------------|-----------|
| **Programming language** | Java (Standard Edition) | Python, C, Go, Kotlin | Java is the dominant enterprise language; well-known entry-point conventions; platform-independent via JVM |
| **Runtime** | JVM (any version ‚â• 8) | Native compilation (GraalVM), Docker container | JVM is universally available on developer machines; zero config overhead |
| **Build tool** | None (manual `javac` / `java`) | Maven, Gradle, Ant | Application is a single file with zero dependencies ‚Äî a build tool would be over-engineering |
| **Dependency management** | None required | Maven Central, Gradle deps | No third-party libraries used; `java.lang` is always available automatically |
| **Output mechanism** | `System.out.println()` | `Logger`, file output, GUI | Standard output is the simplest, most universally accessible channel for a CLI program |
| **Package structure** | Default (unnamed) package | Named package (e.g. `com.example`) | Single-class application; named packages only needed when multiple classes/modules exist |
| **Version control** | Git / GitHub | SVN, local only | Standard industry practice; enables collaboration and CI/CD pipelines |

### 4.2 Top-Level Decomposition Strategy

The application follows the **Single Responsibility Principle** at its most fundamental level:

```mermaid
mindmap
  root((HelloWorld\nApplication))
    Compilation
      javac compiler
      Single .java file
      Produces .class bytecode
    Execution
      JVM bootstrap
      main() entry point
      System.out write
    Output
      stdout stream
      Console rendering
      Human-readable text
    Version_Control
      Git repository
      GitHub hosting
      Source-only tracking
```

### 4.3 Approaches to Achieve Quality Goals

| Quality Goal | Approach |
|-------------|----------|
| **Simplicity** | One class, one method, one statement ‚Äî zero abstraction layers beyond what Java requires |
| **Correctness** | Hard-coded, deterministic output; no user input, no parsing, no external state ‚Äî cannot produce incorrect output |
| **Portability** | Pure `java.lang` usage; no OS-specific APIs; runs identically on Windows, macOS, and Linux |
| **Maintainability** | Clear class/method naming; Java convention-compliant structure; easy to add methods or packages alongside |
| **Learnability** | Canonical Java "Hello World" pattern, recognised by every Java developer worldwide |

---

## 5. Building Block View

> **Source analysis**: `HelloWorld.java` ‚Äî 1 class, 1 method, 1 statement.
> Full AST decomposition of the entire application.

### 5.1 Level 1 ‚Äî Whitebox: Whole System

At the highest level, the entire system is a single deployable unit: the `HelloWorld` application.
It has no internal sub-systems, modules, or packages beyond the default Java package.

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph System["HelloWorld Application (copilot-test-ktruchcz)"]
        direction TB
        HW["üü¶ HelloWorld\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nThe sole class.\nContains the program\nentry point."]
    end

    stdin_in["‚å®Ô∏è Command-line args\n(String[] args)"]
    stdout_out["üñ•Ô∏è Console stdout\n'Hello World'"]

    stdin_in -->|"passed to main()"| HW
    HW -->|"System.out.println()"| stdout_out

    style System fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#3b82f6,stroke-width:2px,color:#1e3a5f
    style HW fill:#3b82f6,color:#ffffff,stroke:#1d4ed8
    style stdin_in fill:#f1f5f9,stroke:#94a3b8,color:#334155
    style stdout_out fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#22c55e,color:#14532d
```

**Contained building blocks**:

| Block | Type | Responsibility |
|-------|------|----------------|
| `HelloWorld` | Java class (public) | Sole class; owns program entry point |
| `main(String[] args)` | Static method | JVM entry point; orchestrates all program behaviour |

**Important interfaces**:

| Interface | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `main(String[] args)` | Method signature | JVM-standard entry point; invoked by `java HelloWorld` |
| `System.out` | `java.io.PrintStream` | External channel for writing text to stdout |

### 5.2 Level 2 ‚Äî Whitebox: `HelloWorld` Class

```mermaid
classDiagram
    direction TB

    class HelloWorld {
        <<Application Entry Point>>
        +main(args : String[]) void$
    }

    class SystemOut {
        <<java.io.PrintStream ‚Äî external>>
        +println(x : String) void
    }

    class JVMRuntime {
        <<java.lang ‚Äî external>>
        +bootstrap() void$
    }

    JVMRuntime --> HelloWorld : "invokes main()"
    HelloWorld --> SystemOut : "calls println()"

    note for HelloWorld "Single public class.\nDefault (unnamed) package.\nNo constructors, fields,\nor instance methods."
    note for SystemOut "Accessed via System.out\nstatic field ‚Äî part of\nJava standard library."
```

### 5.3 Level 3 ‚Äî Whitebox: `main()` Method (Statement Level)

A breakdown of every statement and expression inside `main()`:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A["‚ñ∂ JVM calls\nmain(String[] args)"]
    B["Evaluate expression:\nSystem.out"]
    C["Evaluate expression:\n'Hello World'"]
    D["Invoke method:\nSystem.out.println('Hello World')"]
    E["Write bytes to\nstdout stream"]
    F["Append newline\ncharacter '\\n'"]
    G["‚óÄ main() returns void\nJVM exits with code 0"]

    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G

    style A fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff,stroke:#1d4ed8
    style D fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff,stroke:#6d28d9
    style E fill:#10b981,color:#fff,stroke:#059669
    style G fill:#6b7280,color:#fff,stroke:#374151
```

### 5.4 Source Code Reference

```java
// File: HelloWorld.java
// Package: (default)
// Lines: 5

public class HelloWorld {                          // L1 ‚Äì class declaration
    public static void main(String[] args) {       // L2 ‚Äì JVM entry point
        System.out.println("Hello World");         // L3 ‚Äì sole statement: write to stdout
    }                                              // L4 ‚Äì end main()
}                                                  // L5 ‚Äì end class
```

**Code metrics**:

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Lines of Code (total) | 5 |
| Lines of Code (executable) | 1 |
| Classes | 1 |
| Methods | 1 |
| Fields / Attributes | 0 |
| Constructors | 0 (default implicit) |
| Cyclomatic Complexity | 1 (no branches) |
| External dependencies | 0 (java.lang implicit) |
| Packages | 1 (default) |

## 6. Runtime View

> **Source analysis**: Execution flow derived from `HelloWorld.java` ‚Äî single `main()` call
> path with no branching, loops, or exception handlers.

### 6.1 Scenario 1 ‚Äî Normal Execution (Happy Path)

This is the only runtime scenario for this application. There are no alternative paths,
error conditions from user input, or conditional branches.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Developer as üë§ Developer
    participant OS as üñ•Ô∏è Operating System
    participant JVM as ‚öôÔ∏è JVM / ClassLoader
    participant HW as üü¶ HelloWorld
    participant Stdout as üì§ System.out (stdout)
    participant Terminal as üñ•Ô∏è Terminal

    Developer->>OS: java HelloWorld
    activate OS

    OS->>JVM: launch JVM process
    activate JVM

    JVM->>JVM: load HelloWorld.class
    JVM->>JVM: verify bytecode
    JVM->>JVM: initialise class HelloWorld

    JVM->>HW: invoke main(String[] args)
    activate HW

    HW->>Stdout: System.out.println("Hello World")
    activate Stdout

    Stdout->>Terminal: write bytes "Hello World\n"
    activate Terminal
    Terminal-->>Developer: display "Hello World"
    deactivate Terminal

    Stdout-->>HW: (returns void)
    deactivate Stdout

    HW-->>JVM: main() returns void
    deactivate HW

    JVM->>JVM: run shutdown hooks (none)
    JVM->>OS: exit(0)
    deactivate JVM

    OS-->>Developer: process exit code 0
    deactivate OS
```

### 6.2 Scenario 2 ‚Äî Compilation Phase

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Developer as üë§ Developer
    participant FS as üìÅ File System
    participant Javac as üîß javac Compiler (JDK)

    Developer->>Javac: javac HelloWorld.java
    activate Javac

    Javac->>FS: read HelloWorld.java
    FS-->>Javac: source text (5 lines)

    Javac->>Javac: lexical analysis (tokenise)
    Javac->>Javac: syntax analysis (parse AST)
    Javac->>Javac: semantic analysis (type check)
    Javac->>Javac: code generation (bytecode)

    Javac->>FS: write HelloWorld.class
    note over FS: HelloWorld.class is\ngit-ignored (.gitignore)

    Javac-->>Developer: exit 0 (success)
    deactivate Javac
```

### 6.3 Runtime Lifecycle Flowchart

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Start(["üöÄ Developer runs\njava HelloWorld"])

    C1{"HelloWorld.class\nexists?"}
    Compile["Run:\njavac HelloWorld.java"]
    CompileOK{"Compilation\nsucceeded?"}
    CompileErr(["‚ùå Compiler error\n(syntax / classpath issue)"])

    Load["JVM loads\nHelloWorld.class"]
    Init["JVM initialises\nHelloWorld class"]
    Invoke["JVM invokes\nmain(String[] args)"]
    Print["System.out.println\n('Hello World')"]
    Output(["‚úÖ 'Hello World'\nprinted to terminal"])
    Exit(["üèÅ JVM exits\nwith code 0"])

    Start --> C1
    C1 -- No --> Compile
    C1 -- Yes --> Load
    Compile --> CompileOK
    CompileOK -- No --> CompileErr
    CompileOK -- Yes --> Load
    Load --> Init
    Init --> Invoke
    Invoke --> Print
    Print --> Output
    Output --> Exit

    style Start fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff,stroke:#1d4ed8
    style Output fill:#22c55e,color:#fff,stroke:#15803d
    style Exit fill:#6b7280,color:#fff,stroke:#374151
    style CompileErr fill:#ef4444,color:#fff,stroke:#b91c1c
```

### 6.4 Runtime Characteristics

| Characteristic | Value |
|----------------|-------|
| **Startup time** | ~50‚Äì300 ms (JVM cold start) |
| **Execution time** | < 1 ms (single println) |
| **Memory footprint** | ~10‚Äì30 MB (JVM base heap) |
| **CPU usage** | Negligible (single write) |
| **Threads** | 1 (main thread) + JVM system threads |
| **Network I/O** | None |
| **File I/O** | None (after class loading) |
| **Exit code** | `0` (success, always) |
| **Idempotency** | ‚úÖ Fully idempotent ‚Äî always prints same output |
| **Determinism** | ‚úÖ Fully deterministic ‚Äî no random/time/input dependency |

## 7. Deployment View

> **Source analysis**: No Dockerfile, CI/CD config, or infrastructure-as-code found.
> Deployment topology inferred from Java project structure and `.gitignore`.

### 7.1 Infrastructure Overview

The HelloWorld application requires no server infrastructure, containerisation, or cloud
services. The deployment topology is entirely local to a single machine.

```mermaid

        subgraph Source_Layer["üìÅ Source Layer"]
            git_repo["Git Repository\ncopilot-test-ktruchcz\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nHelloWorld.java\nREADME.md\n.gitignore"]
        end

        subgraph Build_Layer["üîß Build Layer (JDK)"]

            class_file["HelloWorld.class\n(compiled bytecode)\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\ngit-ignored"]

        subgraph Runtime_Layer["‚öôÔ∏è Runtime Layer (JRE)"]

            stdout_stream["stdout stream\n(System.out)\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\njava.io.PrintStream"]
        end

        subgraph Output_Layer["üì∫ Output Layer"]
            terminal["Terminal / Shell\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nbash / zsh / cmd\nPowerShell"]

    end

    end

    javac_tool -->|"produces"| class_file
    class_file -->|"java HelloWorld"| jvm_proc
    jvm_proc -->|"println()"| stdout_stream
    stdout_stream -->|"Hello World\n"| terminal

    git_repo <-->|"git push / pull"| gh_repo
```

### 7.3 Environment Requirements

| Environment | Requirement | Minimum Version |
|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
| **Operating System** | Any OS supporting a JRE | Windows 7, macOS 10.9, Linux kernel 2.6 |
| **Java Development Kit** | Required for compilation | JDK 8 (for `javac`) |
| **Java Runtime Environment** | Required for execution | JRE 8 (for `java`) |
| **Disk Space (source)** | `HelloWorld.java` | < 1 KB |
| **Disk Space (bytecode)** | `HelloWorld.class` | ~300 bytes |
| **RAM** | JVM base footprint | ~10 MB minimum |
| **Network** | Not required | N/A |
| **Database** | Not required | N/A |
| **Container runtime** | Not required | N/A |

### 7.4 Potential Future Deployment Targets

| Target | What Would Change |
|--------|-------------------|
| **Docker container** | Add `Dockerfile` with `openjdk` base image; `CMD ["java", "HelloWorld"]` |
| **GitHub Actions CI** | Add `.github/workflows/build.yml`; steps: `javac` ‚Üí `java` |
| **Maven/Gradle project** | Add `pom.xml` or `build.gradle`; enables IDE integration and test runners |
| **Executable JAR** | `jar cfe HelloWorld.jar HelloWorld HelloWorld.class` |

## 8. Cross-cutting Concepts

> **Source analysis**: Cross-cutting concerns identified from `HelloWorld.java` structure,
> Java language rules, and absence of supporting infrastructure files.

### 8.1 Domain Model

The domain model for this application is trivially simple ‚Äî there are no domain entities,
value objects, aggregates, or services beyond the greeting string itself.

```mermaid
erDiagram
    HELLO_WORLD_APPLICATION {
        string greeting "Hello World"
        string entryPoint "main(String[] args)"
    }
    OUTPUT_CHANNEL {
        string type "stdout"
        string implementation "java.io.PrintStream"
        string accessor "System.out"
    }
    RUNTIME_ENVIRONMENT {
        string name "JVM"
        string version "8+"
        string platform "Cross-platform"
    }

    HELLO_WORLD_APPLICATION ||--|| OUTPUT_CHANNEL : "writes to"
    HELLO_WORLD_APPLICATION }|--|| RUNTIME_ENVIRONMENT : "executes within"
```

### 8.2 Logging and Observability

| Aspect | Current Implementation | Recommended Future State |
|--------|----------------------|--------------------------|
| **Application logging** | `System.out.println()` ‚Äî direct stdout write, no structured logging | Add `java.util.logging` or SLF4J + Logback |
| **Log levels** | None ‚Äî single unconditional print | `INFO`, `DEBUG`, `ERROR` levels |
| **Log format** | Plain text: `Hello World\n` | Structured: timestamp, level, message |
| **Error logging** | None ‚Äî no `try/catch`, no `System.err` usage | Log to `System.err` for error conditions |
| **Metrics / tracing** | None | Micrometer / OpenTelemetry for production |

### 8.3 Error Handling

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A["main() starts"]
    B["System.out.println()"]
    C{"PrintStream\navailable?"}
    D["Output written\nsuccessfully"]
    E["NullPointerException\n(System.out is null)"]
    F["JVM terminates\nwith uncaught exception"]

    A --> B
    B --> C
    C -- "Yes (normal case)" --> D
    C -- "No (System.out reassigned to null)" --> E
    E --> F

    note1["‚öÝÔ∏è Current code has NO\nexplicit error handling.\nAny exception propagates\ndirectly to JVM."]

    style D fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
    style E fill:#ef4444,color:#fff
    style F fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style note1 fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#f59e0b,color:#78350f
```

**Current error handling assessment**:
- ‚úÖ For this application's scope, no error handling is needed ‚Äî `System.out` is always available under normal JVM operation
- ‚öÝÔ∏è No `try-catch` blocks present ‚Äî any unexpected runtime exception will produce an unformatted stack trace
- ‚ÑπÔ∏è `main()` has no checked exception declaration ‚Äî no `throws` clause required as `println()` does not throw checked exceptions

### 8.4 Security Concepts

| Security Aspect | Status | Notes |
|-----------------|--------|-------|
| **Input validation** | N/A | `args` array is accepted but never read or used |
| **Output sanitisation** | N/A | Output is a hard-coded string literal ‚Äî no injection risk |
| **Authentication / Authorisation** | N/A | No user accounts, sessions, or access control |
| **Secrets / Credentials** | None present | No API keys, passwords, or tokens in source |
| **Dependency vulnerabilities** | None | Zero third-party dependencies |
| **Network exposure** | None | Application opens no sockets or ports |

### 8.5 Internationalisation (i18n)

| Aspect | Current State |
|--------|---------------|
| **Language** | Hard-coded English: `"Hello World"` |
| **Character encoding** | JVM default platform encoding for stdout |
| **Locale** | Not considered ‚Äî no `Locale` or `ResourceBundle` used |
| **Future i18n** | Would require `ResourceBundle` + properties files per locale |

### 8.6 Design Patterns Applied

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph Applied["‚úÖ Patterns Applied"]
        P1["Single Entry Point\nPattern\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nmain() as the\nsole entry point"]
        P2["Facade (implicit)\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nSystem.out as a\nsimplified facade\nover stdout I/O"]
    end

    subgraph NotNeeded["‚ûñ Not Applicable (by design)"]
        N1["Factory / Builder\n(no objects created)"]
        N2["Singleton / DI\n(no services)"]
        N3["Observer / Event\n(no events)"]
        N4["MVC / Layered\n(no UI/data layer)"]
    end

    style Applied fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#22c55e,color:#14532d
    style NotNeeded fill:#f1f5f9,stroke:#94a3b8,color:#475569
```

### 8.7 Build and Configuration Management

| Aspect | Current State | Recommendation |
|--------|---------------|----------------|
| **Build tool** | None ‚Äî manual `javac` | Add Maven or Gradle for reproducible builds |
| **Configuration files** | None | No `application.properties` needed at current scope |
| **Environment variables** | None used | N/A |
| **Feature flags** | None | N/A |
| **Version pinning** | No JDK version specified | Add `.java-version` or `pom.xml` `<source>`/`<target>` |

## 9. Architecture Decisions

> Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) document the key choices made in building this
> system, the context behind them, and their consequences.

### ADR-001 ‚Äî Use Java as the Implementation Language

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | Project inception |
| **Deciders** | Repository owner |

**Context**: A programming language must be chosen to implement the HelloWorld application.

**Decision**: Use **Java Standard Edition** as the sole implementation language.

**Rationale**:
- Java is one of the most widely taught and used languages globally
- Provides a well-defined, universally-known entry-point convention (`main(String[] args)`)
- Platform-independent via the JVM ‚Äî "write once, run anywhere"
- Strong static typing catches errors at compile time
- Zero learning curve for any Java developer

**Consequences**:
- ‚úÖ Maximum portability across operating systems
- ‚úÖ Immediate recognisability for Java developers
- ‚úÖ Strong IDE and tooling support
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Requires JDK/JRE installation (not zero-dependency like a shell script)
- ‚öÝÔ∏è JVM cold-start overhead (~100‚Äì300 ms) for a program that executes in < 1 ms

---

### ADR-002 ‚Äî No Build Automation Tool

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | Project inception |
| **Deciders** | Repository owner |

**Context**: Java projects typically use Maven, Gradle, or Ant for build automation,
dependency management, and test execution.

**Decision**: Use **no build tool** ‚Äî compile and run manually via `javac` and `java`.

**Rationale**:
- Single source file with zero external dependencies
- A `pom.xml` or `build.gradle` would be significantly larger than the application itself
- No dependency versions to manage
- Simpler onboarding ‚Äî any developer with a JDK can compile immediately

**Consequences**:
- ‚úÖ Zero build configuration overhead
- ‚úÖ Fastest possible onboarding for new developers
- ‚öÝÔ∏è No automated testing pipeline without additional tooling
- ‚öÝÔ∏è No reproducible build guarantees (no pinned JDK version)
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Does not scale ‚Äî a build tool would be required if any dependency is added

---

### ADR-003 ‚Äî Output via `System.out.println()`

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | Project inception |
| **Deciders** | Repository owner |

**Context**: The application must display text output. Multiple output mechanisms exist in Java.

**Decision**: Use **`System.out.println()`** for output.

**Alternatives considered**:

| Alternative | Reason Not Chosen |
|-------------|------------------|
| `java.util.logging.Logger` | Over-engineered for a single-message program |
| SLF4J + Logback | Requires external dependencies |
| `System.err.println()` | stderr is for errors, not normal output |
| Writing to a file | Requires file system access ‚Äî not needed |
| GUI (`JFrame`, JavaFX) | Heavyweight framework for a one-liner |

**Consequences**:
- ‚úÖ Simplest possible implementation
- ‚úÖ No dependencies required
- ‚úÖ Output is visible immediately in any terminal
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Not structured/parseable ‚Äî would need replacement for machine-readable output
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Cannot be redirected independently of other stdout output without shell redirection

---

### ADR-004 ‚Äî Default (Unnamed) Java Package

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | Project inception |
| **Deciders** | Repository owner |

**Context**: Java classes can be organised into named packages (e.g. `com.example.helloworld`)
or placed in the default unnamed package.

**Decision**: Place `HelloWorld` in the **default package** (no `package` statement).

**Rationale**: Named packages are a best practice when building reusable libraries or
multi-class applications. For a single-class demonstration program, the overhead of a
package hierarchy is unnecessary.

**Consequences**:
- ‚úÖ No package declaration required ‚Äî one less line of boilerplate
- ‚úÖ Directly runnable: `java HelloWorld` (no fully-qualified name needed)
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Classes in the default package cannot be imported by classes in named packages
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Not suitable if the project grows into a library or multi-module application

---

### ADR-005 ‚Äî Exclude Compiled Artifacts from Version Control

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | Project inception |
| **Deciders** | Repository owner |

**Context**: `javac` produces `.class` binary files alongside source. These can be committed
to Git or excluded.

**Decision**: Exclude `*.class` files via `.gitignore`.

**Consequences**:
- ‚úÖ Repository contains only human-readable source
- ‚úÖ Smaller repository size
- ‚úÖ No merge conflicts on binary files
- ‚öÝÔ∏è Recipients must compile the source themselves before running

---

## 10. Quality Requirements

> **Source analysis**: Quality attributes assessed from `HelloWorld.java` code metrics,
> structural analysis, and absence of testing/CI infrastructure.

### 10.1 Quality Tree

```mermaid
graph TD
    Root["üå≥ Quality\nRequirements"]

    Root --> Func["‚öôÔ∏è Functional\nSuitability"]
    Root --> Perf["‚ö° Performance\nEfficiency"]
    Root --> Main["üîß Maintainability"]
    Root --> Port["üåê Portability"]
    Root --> Rely["üõ°Ô∏è Reliability"]
    Root --> Secu["üîí Security"]

    Func --> F1["‚úÖ Functional\nCompleteness\nPrints 'Hello World'"]
    Func --> F2["‚úÖ Functional\nCorrectness\nDeterministic output"]
    Func --> F3["‚úÖ Functional\nAppropriateness\nSingle responsibility"]

    Perf --> P1["‚úÖ Time Behaviour\n< 1ms execution"]
    Perf --> P2["‚úÖ Resource\nUtilisation\nMinimal memory"]

    Main --> M1["‚úÖ Analysability\n5 LOC, CC=1"]
    Main --> M2["‚úÖ Modifiability\nClear extension points"]
    Main --> M3["‚úÖ Testability\nDeterministic, no side effects"]
    Main --> M4["‚öÝÔ∏è No tests\npresent"]

    Port --> Po1["‚úÖ Adaptability\nJVM cross-platform"]
    Port --> Po2["‚úÖ Installability\nJDK only required"]

    Rely --> R1["‚úÖ Fault Tolerance\nNo failure modes"]
    Rely --> R2["‚öÝÔ∏è Recoverability\nNo error handling"]

    Secu --> S1["‚úÖ No attack surface\nNo input, no network"]

    style Root fill:#1d4ed8,color:#fff,stroke:#1e40af

    style Main fill:#7c3aed,color:#fff
    style Port fill:#0891b2,color:#fff
    style Rely fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style Secu fill:#4b5563,color:#fff
```

### 10.2 Quality Scenarios

Quality scenarios follow the format: *Stimulus ‚Üí System Response ‚Üí Metric*.

#### Functional Suitability

| ID | Quality Attribute | Stimulus | Response | Metric | Status |
|----|------------------|---------|----------|--------|--------|
| QS-01 | Functional Correctness | Developer runs `java HelloWorld` | Application prints exactly `Hello World` followed by newline | Output matches expected string 100% of the time | ‚úÖ Met |
| QS-02 | Functional Completeness | Any invocation of `main()` | All defined behaviour (print greeting) is executed | 1/1 requirements implemented | ‚úÖ Met |

#### Performance Efficiency

| ID | Quality Attribute | Stimulus | Response | Metric | Status |
|----|------------------|---------|----------|--------|--------|
| QS-03 | Time Behaviour | Developer executes `java HelloWorld` | Output appears in terminal | JVM startup: < 500 ms; execution: < 1 ms | ‚úÖ Met |
| QS-04 | Resource Utilisation | Application running | Memory consumed | < 30 MB RAM | ‚úÖ Met |

#### Maintainability

| ID | Quality Attribute | Stimulus | Response | Metric | Status |
|----|------------------|---------|----------|--------|--------|
| QS-05 | Analysability | New developer opens `HelloWorld.java` | Developer understands full system | Time to understand: < 30 seconds | ‚úÖ Met |
| QS-06 | Modifiability | Developer adds a new feature (e.g. accept name as arg) | Code change is localised to `main()` | Lines changed: < 5 | ‚úÖ Met |
| QS-07 | Testability | Test engineer writes a unit test | `main()` output can be captured via `System.setOut()` | Testable without refactoring | ‚úÖ Met |
| QS-08 | **Test Coverage** | CI runs test suite | Tests pass | **0% ‚Äî No tests present** | ‚öÝÔ∏è Not Met |

#### Portability

| ID | Quality Attribute | Stimulus | Response | Metric | Status |
|----|------------------|---------|----------|--------|--------|
| QS-09 | Adaptability | Application run on Windows / macOS / Linux | Identical output on all platforms | Output matches on all 3 major OS families | ‚úÖ Met |
| QS-10 | Installability | Fresh machine with only JRE installed | Application executes successfully | 2 commands: `javac` + `java` | ‚úÖ Met |

## 11. Risks and Technical Debt

> **Source analysis**: Risks and debt items identified from missing infrastructure,
> absence of tests, and lack of build tooling in the `copilot-test-ktruchcz` repository.

### 11.1 Risk Register

```mermaid
quadrantChart
    title Risk Matrix ‚Äî HelloWorld Application
    x-axis "Low Likelihood" --> "High Likelihood"
    y-axis "Low Impact" --> "High Impact"
    quadrant-1 "Monitor"
    quadrant-2 "Mitigate Urgently"
    quadrant-3 "Accept"
    quadrant-4 "Mitigate / Plan"

    No Build Tool: [0.7, 0.4]
    No Tests: [0.6, 0.3]
    JDK Version Unspecified: [0.5, 0.35]
    No CI/CD Pipeline: [0.55, 0.3]
    Default Package: [0.3, 0.5]
    No Javadoc: [0.4, 0.2]
    Hard-coded String: [0.2, 0.2]
```

### 11.2 Identified Risks

| ID | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Category | Mitigation |
|----|------|-----------|--------|----------|------------|
| R-01 | **No automated tests** ‚Äî Regressions cannot be detected automatically | High | Medium | Quality | Add JUnit 5 test class; assert `System.out` output |
| R-02 | **No build tool** ‚Äî Build steps not reproducible across environments | High | Medium | Operations | Add Maven `pom.xml` or Gradle `build.gradle` |
| R-03 | **Unspecified JDK version** ‚Äî Behaviour may differ across Java versions | Medium | Low | Compatibility | Add `.java-version` file or `pom.xml` `<java.version>` property |
| R-04 | **No CI/CD pipeline** ‚Äî Changes not automatically validated | Medium | Low | DevOps | Add `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with `javac` + `java` steps |
| R-05 | **Default package usage** ‚Äî Cannot be imported if project grows | Low | Medium | Architecture | Migrate to named package (e.g. `com.example`) if new classes added |
| R-06 | **No Javadoc** ‚Äî API undocumented at code level | Medium | Low | Documentation | Add `/** */` Javadoc to class and `main()` method |
| R-07 | **Minimal README** ‚Äî Project purpose not explained in repository | High | Low | Documentation | Expand `README.md` with purpose, build, and run instructions |

### 11.3 Technical Debt Items

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph High_Priority["üî¥ High Priority (Should Fix Soon)"]
        TD1["TD-01: No Unit Tests\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nDebt: Missing JUnit tests\nEffort: Low (30 min)\nImpact: Enables regression safety"]
        TD2["TD-02: No Build Tool\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nDebt: Manual compile/run\nEffort: Low (1 hour)\nImpact: Reproducible builds,\nIDE integration"]
    end
    subgraph Medium_Priority["üü° Medium Priority (Plan to Fix)"]
        TD3["TD-03: Minimal README\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nDebt: No usage docs\nEffort: Low (15 min)\nImpact: Developer onboarding"]
        TD4["TD-04: No Javadoc\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nDebt: No inline docs\nEffort: Low (10 min)\nImpact: IDE hover docs"]
        TD5["TD-05: JDK Version\nNot Pinned\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nDebt: Compatibility risk\nEffort: Minimal (5 min)\nImpact: Build consistency"]
    end

        TD7["TD-07: No CI/CD\n‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ\nDebt: No automation\nEffort: Medium (2 hours)\nImpact: Quality gate automation"]
    end

    style High_Priority fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#ef4444,color:#7f1d1d
    style Medium_Priority fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#eab308,color:#713f12

```

### 11.4 Debt Remediation Roadmap

| Sprint | Action | Effort | Benefit |
|--------|--------|--------|---------|
| **Immediate** | Expand `README.md` with build and run instructions | 15 min | Better discoverability |
| **Sprint 1** | Add `pom.xml` (Maven) with `java.version` 11 or 17 | 1 hour | Reproducible builds, IDE support |
| **Sprint 1** | Add JUnit 5 test: `HelloWorldTest.java` | 30 min | Regression detection |
| **Sprint 2** | Add `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (compile + test) | 2 hours | Automated quality gate |
| **Sprint 2** | Add Javadoc to class and `main()` | 10 min | Code-level documentation |
| **Future** | Migrate to named package if project grows | 1 hour | Reusability, maintainability |

## 12. Glossary

> Domain terms, Java technical terms, and architecture terms used throughout this document.

### 12.1 Domain Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Hello World** | The canonical first program in any programming language; by convention it prints the text "Hello World" (or "Hello, World!") to demonstrate a working development environment |
| **copilot-test-ktruchcz** | The name of the GitHub repository hosting this application |
| **Greeting** | The string output produced by this application: `"Hello World"` |

### 12.2 Java Technical Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Java SE** | Java Standard Edition ‚Äî the core Java platform for general-purpose programming |
| **JDK** | Java Development Kit ‚Äî includes the compiler (`javac`), runtime (`java`), and standard libraries needed to develop Java applications |
| **JRE** | Java Runtime Environment ‚Äî a subset of the JDK containing only what is needed to *run* (not compile) Java programs |
| **JVM** | Java Virtual Machine ‚Äî the runtime engine that executes Java bytecode; provides platform independence |
| **`javac`** | The Java compiler; converts `.java` source files into `.class` bytecode files |
| **`java`** | The Java launcher; starts the JVM and invokes the specified class's `main()` method |
| **Bytecode** | The intermediate compiled format produced by `javac` and stored in `.class` files; executed by the JVM |
| **`.class` file** | The compiled binary output of `javac`; contains JVM bytecode |
| **`main(String[] args)`** | The standard JVM entry-point method signature; execution begins here when `java ClassName` is run |
| **`System.out`** | A static field of type `java.io.PrintStream` representing the standard output stream |
| **`System.out.println()`** | Writes a string followed by a newline character to stdout |
| **`stdout`** | Standard Output ‚Äî the default output channel for a process (file descriptor 1); displayed in the terminal |
| **`java.lang`** | The core Java package (e.g. `String`, `System`, `Object`); automatically imported in every Java program |
| **Default package** | The unnamed package in Java; used when no `package` statement is present in a source file |
| **PascalCase** | A naming convention where each word starts with an uppercase letter (e.g. `HelloWorld`); required for Java class names |
| **Cyclomatic Complexity** | A software metric measuring the number of linearly independent paths through code; value of 1 means no branches |
| **Static method** | A method belonging to the class itself rather than an instance; `main()` must be `static` for JVM invocation |
| **`public` access modifier** | Makes a class or method accessible from any other class; required for `main()` and the `HelloWorld` class |
| **`void` return type** | Indicates a method returns no value; required for `main()` |

### 12.3 Architecture and Documentation Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Arc42** | A lightweight, pragmatic template for software architecture documentation; consists of 12 structured sections |
| **ADR** | Architecture Decision Record ‚Äî a document capturing a significant architectural decision, its context, and consequences |
| **C4 Model** | A hierarchical set of diagrams (Context, Container, Component, Code) for describing software architecture |
| **Building Block** | An Arc42 term for any structural element of the system (class, package, module, service) |
| **Runtime View** | An Arc42 section describing the dynamic behaviour of the system during execution |
| **Cross-cutting Concern** | An aspect of a system that affects multiple components and cannot be cleanly encapsulated (e.g. logging, security, error handling) |
| **Technical Debt** | The implied cost of future rework caused by choosing a quick or incomplete solution now |
| **CI/CD** | Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery ‚Äî automated pipelines for building, testing, and deploying software |
| **LOC** | Lines of Code ‚Äî a basic measure of program size |
| **Mermaid** | A JavaScript-based diagramming tool that renders diagrams from text-based syntax; used for all diagrams in this document |

---

## Appendix: Quick Reference

### Compile and Run

```bash
# Prerequisites: JDK 8+ installed

# Step 1 ‚Äî Compile
javac HelloWorld.java

# Step 2 ‚Äî Run
java HelloWorld

# Expected output:
# Hello World
```

### Source Code (Complete)

```java
public class HelloWorld {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("Hello World");
    }
}
```
